message: bd sync: apply DB changes after import

files.patch: @@ -405,7 +405,7 @@ {"id":"brenner_bot-augk.2","title":"Task: Add session start flags + store binding in artifact metadata","description":"# Goal Implement the first usable form of session→target binding. --- # CLI requirements - `./brenner.ts session start` accepts: - `--target-repo ` (required for experiment mode) - optional: `--target-branch`, `--target-remote`, `--results-path`, `--experiment-command` - The kickoff message body includes a clear "Target repo" block. --- # Artifact requirements - Compiled artifacts should include binding metadata in YAML front matter, so later readers know: - what repo was being studied - which revision/branch (if provided) --- # Acceptance criteria - [ ] `session start` supports `--target-repo` - [ ] `session compile/write/publish` preserve binding metadata into YAML front matter - [ ] Unit tests cover flag parsing and metadata persistence ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:01:41.992505748-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T19:33:09.487337884-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T19:33:09.487337884-05:00","close_reason":"Superseded by v0 decision: use Agent Mail project_key as the research workspace (start sessions in the repo/folder being studied); avoid cross-repo target binding in v0.","labels":["binding","cli","experiments"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-augk.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-augk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:01:42.020732228-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-augk.3","title":"Task: Cross-repo file reservations (optional, advisory)","description":"# Goal Allow the operator/orchestrator to reserve files in the target repo via Agent Mail, without moving the main thread out of BrennerBot's project. --- # Background File reservations prevent agent clobbering, but Agent Mail reservations are scoped to a `project_key`. For cross-repo sessions we likely need to reserve paths in: - BrennerBot repo (artifacts, specs) - Target repo (code under study) --- # Proposed approach - Ensure the target repo is also an Agent Mail "project" (ensure_project). - Register the same agent identity name in the target project. - Call `file_reservation_paths(project_key=, paths=[...])`. --- # Risks / considerations - This creates a second Agent Mail mailbox archive (in the target repo), but we will NOT use it for conversation. - Needs careful UX so operators understand reservations are advisory. --- # Acceptance criteria - [ ] CLI can reserve a path glob in the target repo project - [ ] Failure is fail-soft (warn, do not break session) unless explicitly required ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:01:55.767749794-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T19:33:09.488664772-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T19:33:09.488664772-05:00","close_reason":"Superseded by v0 decision: use Agent Mail project_key as the research workspace (start sessions in the repo/folder being studied); avoid cross-repo target binding in v0.","labels":["agent-mail","experiments","file-reservations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-augk.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-augk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:01:55.789236605-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-b04","title":"Task: Configure test coverage reporting","description":"Set up test coverage collection and reporting. ## Requirements 1. Coverage thresholds (warn at 60%, fail at 40%) 2. Coverage report generation (lcov, html) 3. Coverage diff on PRs 4. Badge generation for README ## Tools - @vitest/coverage-v8 for collection - GitHub Actions for reporting - Optional: Codecov or similar service ## Output - Coverage report in CI artifacts - Coverage summary in PR comments - Coverage badge for README","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:12:17.89716331-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:42:52.535792674-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:42:52.535792674-05:00","close_reason":"Coverage already configured in vitest.config.ts with v8 provider, lcov/html reporters, and 50% thresholds","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-b04","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-djz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:12:31.086219282-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-b04","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-0fs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:12:31.215251062-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} -{"id":"brenner_bot-b1uy","title":"TASK: Offline Mode & Network Resilience","description":"Handle network failures gracefully. Include: Service worker for offline access, queued actions when offline, reconnection detection and sync, clear UI indicators for offline state, graceful degradation when Agent Mail is unavailable. Users may work on planes or with spotty connections.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:35:39.275156024-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T09:30:28.188271542-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-05T09:30:28.188271542-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented offline UX with service worker, queue + auto-sync, and UI indicators for offline state.","labels":["brenner-loop","infrastructure"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-b1uy","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1v26.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:36:14.501340496-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"brenner_bot-b1uy","title":"TASK: Offline Mode & Network Resilience","description":"Handle network failures gracefully. Include: Service worker for offline access, queued actions when offline, reconnection detection and sync, clear UI indicators for offline state, graceful degradation when Agent Mail is unavailable. Users may work on planes or with spotty connections.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:35:39.275156024-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T09:31:10.376049195-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-05T09:31:10.376049195-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented offline resilience: service worker + offline fallback page, queued session kickoffs/actions when offline, network status badge + auto-flush on reconnect.","labels":["brenner-loop","infrastructure"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-b1uy","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1v26.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:36:14.501340496-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-b2o","title":"TanStack Form: Upgrade Session Creation Form","description":"# Goal Upgrade the session creation form to use TanStack Form with Zod validation for better UX and maintainability. ## Background The session creation form (/sessions/new) has 4 fields: - Thread ID (required, unique identifier format) - Hypothesis (required, text) - Prior Beliefs (required, text) - Target Agent (required, selection) Current implementation uses native HTML form with server action: ```typescript
